title: Garlicky Kissed Brazilian Salmon Bowl
description: A nourishing and flavorful Brazilian dish featuring tender salmon, garlic, and herbs, served in a cozy lacquerware bowl. This gluten-free-friendly recipe is perfect for a hearty dinner or special occasion.
instructions: Season the salmon fillets with salt, black pepper, and paprika.
In a small bowl, mix together lime juice, olive oil, garlic, and rice vinegar.
Place the salmon fillets in a baking dish and pour the lime juice mixture over them.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake for 12 minutes.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 8-10 minutes, or until the salmon is cooked through.
Meanwhile, heat a small skillet over medium heat and sauté the grated ginger for 1-2 minutes, or until fragrant.
Add the chopped cilantro and parsley to the skillet and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, or until wilted.
To serve, place a salmon fillet on each plate and top with the sautéed herbs and ginger.
Garnish with additional cilantro and parsley, if desired.
If using, sprinkle with Brazilian cheese and serve immediately.
Serve hot in a cozy lacquerware bowl.
Garnish with lime wedges, if desired.
